title: Find the unpaired number
planQuestions: What property identifies the unpaired value?
How will zero be treated by your check?
Which input proves a negative value is not skipped?

hints.text: Treat every numeric value as data, including zero.
hints.concept: A value being zero does not make it absent.

hints.text: What information would let you ask how many times each value occurred?
hints.concept: Occurrence counts separate paired and unpaired values.
hints.lookAt: The selection rule for the returned value.

hints.text: Inspect the condition that chooses the first unique number.
hints.concept: The current selection rule has an implicit truthiness assumption.
hints.lookAt: The function that finds the unpaired number.
hints.testIdea: Use zero as the only value that appears once.

explainBackQuestion: Why is a truthiness check unsafe when zero is a valid number?

brief.story: A batch of readings contains matching duplicate values and one value that appeared only once. Zero and negative readings are still real readings, not missing data.
brief.desiredBehavior: Return the one number that occurs once, including when that number is zero or negative.
acceptanceCriteria: The one unpaired number is returned.
Negative values are handled without special treatment.
constraints: Work in the TypeScript Kata Lab library.
Return null only when no unique number exists.
